templates.text: The shrine does not know it has been abandoned. It continues to wait, in the way only stone can wait — without patience, without impatience, without end.
You kneel at what was once sacred and find it still has something to offer. Forgotten does not mean empty.
Offerings from a dozen seekers before you, mostly dust now. The shrine accepted them all without comment.
Something still lives in this alcove — not a creature, not a spirit, but the accumulated weight of belief, pressed thin by centuries.
The shrine marks you. Not visibly. Not in any way you could verify. But the dungeon's records have been updated.
The carved inscription has been worn smooth by hands that are gone. You trace the outline of what it once said.
Shrines remember everyone who visited. You are now part of what this place remembers.
Old candles. Old ash. A space where something was venerated that no living thing still names.
The shrine's power has not diminished. Only the understanding of it has been lost. Those are very different things.
